role: Minister of Communications and Information Technology (2009-2012); principlist politician; EU-sanctioned for internet censorship
bio:
Reza Taghipour Anvari is an Iranian principlist politician who served as Minister of Communications and Information Technology in [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad)'s second cabinet from 2009 to 2012.

As minister, Taghipour oversaw the [Ministry of Communications](/orgs/ministry-of-communications) during one of the most repressive periods for communications freedom in Iran. His ministry cut off mobile phone and SMS services the day before the [2009 presidential election](/events/presidential-election-iran-2009) for 40 days, blocked social media networks, and jammed satellite television broadcasts during the [Green Movement protests](/events/green-movement-protests-2009).

The European Union sanctioned Taghipour on 23 March 2012 for his role in excessive censorship, imposing a visa ban and asset freeze. His tenure saw the development of Iran's National Information Network, a state-controlled domestic internet infrastructure designed to enable further filtering of international content.
editorial_notes: Born 1957 in Maragheh. EU sanctioned March 2012 for censorship. Not confirmed IRGC member despite some reports of affiliation.
